sanskrit_devanagari:
चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः ।
तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥ १३ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
cātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛṣṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ |
tasya kartāram api māṁ viddhy akartāram avyayam || 13 ||

translations.verbatim_quote: According to the three modes of material nature and the work associated with them, the four divisions of human society are created by Me. And although I am the creator of this system, you should know that I am yet the nondoer, being unchangeable.

According to the three modes of material nature and the work associated with them, the four divisions of human society are created by Me. And although I am the creator of this system, you should know that I am yet the nondoer, being unchangeable.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2125 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: The Lord is the creator of everything. Everything is born of Him, everything is sustained by Him, and everything, after annihilation, rests in Him. He is therefore the creator of the four divisions of the social order, beginning with the intelligent class of men, technically called brāhmaṇas due to…

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.13 Catur-varnyam-meaning the same as catvarah varnah, the four castes; srstam, have been created; maya, by Me who am God, which accords with such Vedic texts as, ‘The Brahmanas were His face…’ (Rg. 10.90.12); guna-karma-vibhagasah, through a classification of the gunas and duties. [A.G.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-13-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara reads guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ as: the four varṇas were created according to the division of guṇa (sattva, rajas, tamas) and the corresponding karma (action) appropriate to each. This is a typology by intrinsic and acquired qualities, not by birth-line. The akartā / avyaya clause is read in Advaita fashion: the Lord as Brahman is creator only at the empirical level; in essence, he is non-doer.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.13 The whole universe beginning with Brahma and ending with a cluster of grass, with the system of four stations divided according to Sattva and other Gunas and by actions like self-control corresponding to the Gunas, was created by Me. The mention of ‘creation’ is for illustration.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-13-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja reads the verse as the Lord's declaration that the four-fold ordering of human society reflects an underlying division by guṇa and karma — the Lord's creative ordering follows the intrinsic and acquired qualities of beings. The Lord is creator at the cosmic level but, by his nature as the unchanging substrate, is also akartā in the deeper sense.

positions.rendering: the four divisions of human society, according to the modes of material nature and the work attributed to them
the four varṇas, created according to the division of the three guṇas and the karma corresponding to each
the four-fold ordering, reflecting the underlying division by intrinsic guṇa and acquired karma

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: What the verse explicitly says: typology by guṇa and karma. What the verse does NOT say: typology by birth. The historical caste-by-birth reading is a later extension, contested even within tradition. Engineering layers must honor what the verse explicitly says (typology by intrinsic disposition + acquired action) and not smuggle in the birth-based reading. This is a STRETCHED-tag candidate verse if the engineering layer cannot honor both halves cleanly.

literal_meaning: The four-fold typology was created by me according to the division of guṇa and karma; though I am its creator, know me as non-doer, unchanging.
traditional_meaning_consensus: The chapter's most doctrinally fraught verse. Krishna explicitly says cātur-varṇya was created BY GUṆA AND KARMA — by intrinsic disposition (guṇa) and acquired action (karma) — NOT by birth. The historical caste-by-birth reading is an extension; the verse itself says guṇa-karma. The second hemistich adds: though Krishna is the creator of the typology, he is also non-doer (akartā), unchanging (avyaya). Both halves of the verse are loaded — the first by the historical caste-system reading, the second by the metaphysical claim of agency-without-binding.
